index_layer.problem_summary: AI generated code calls non-existent API or imports a package that does not exist

resolution_layer.root_cause: LLM training data contains adjacent but non-identical APIs; model interpolates plausible-but-fake symbols, especially for recent or niche libraries
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Pin documented APIs; verify every imported symbol against the installed package version before relying on it; add a smoke import test in CI; prefer official examples over freshly-typed-by-AI calls
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Replace fabricated symbol with documented equivalent; add unit test that imports and calls the real API once
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Run import against actual installed version in CI; lint with vulture / unimport; verify by reading vendor documentation
abandoned_approaches: Trusting AI suggestion without doc check: leads to runtime errors at production time
Wrapping in try/except to suppress: hides the underlying issue

index_layer.problem_summary: AI generated datetime code mixes local time and UTC, breaking comparison and storage

resolution_layer.root_cause: AI defaults to datetime.now() / new Date() / Calendar.getInstance() without timezone awareness; comparing naive and aware values silently shifts by hours
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Always store and transmit in UTC; convert to local only at the display boundary; forbid naive datetime in domain layer; lint for datetime.now() calls
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Replace datetime.now() with datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc) (or platform equivalent); centralize tz-conversion in a helper
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Add tests crossing DST boundary and across timezones; review all serialization formats include offset
abandoned_approaches: Setting server TZ env var: fragile across deployments
Manually adding/subtracting hours: error-prone and asymmetric across DST

index_layer.problem_summary: AI generated code wraps everything in try/catch and swallows the exception, hiding real failures

resolution_layer.root_cause: LLM optimizes for code that 'compiles and runs' over code that surfaces failures; broad catches make demos look stable while masking bugs in production
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Catch specific exceptions only; always log or rethrow; treat 'making the test pass by suppressing' as a code smell; require justification comment for any broad catch
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Narrow catch clauses; add structured logging in catch blocks; rethrow domain-meaningful errors
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Static analysis rule for empty catch / catch Exception; review all PR-introduced catches in code review
abandoned_approaches: Logging at WARN level only: insufficient signal in production dashboards
Returning default value: silently corrupts downstream computation

index_layer.problem_summary: SwiftUI @State declared in a parent view leaks across child instances, producing stale or shared values

resolution_layer.root_cause: @State persists per view-identity; when the view is reused for a different logical entity (different list row, different page) without identity change, the old state survives
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Move ownership upward via @Binding or environment object; or reset state via .id() modifier when the logical entity changes; never use @State for per-row-data
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Add .id(item.id) to row view, OR replace @State with @Binding driven by parent
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Snapshot test scrolling list and verifying each row reflects its own data; manually scroll and reorder
abandoned_approaches: Calling a reset method in onAppear: unreliable due to view recycling timing
Using @StateObject: same identity issue, just heavier

index_layer.problem_summary: UIKit network completion handler updates UI directly without dispatching to main thread, causing crashes or visual glitches

resolution_layer.root_cause: URLSession completion handlers run on a background queue by default; UIKit requires UI mutation on main thread
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Wrap UI mutations in DispatchQueue.main.async at the boundary; or use @MainActor / Task { @MainActor in ... }; assert main-thread in UI controllers in DEBUG
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Wrap setText/reloadData in DispatchQueue.main.async { ... } or annotate caller with @MainActor
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Enable Main Thread Checker in scheme; manually trigger network response on slow network; instrument with assert(Thread.isMainThread)
abandoned_approaches: performSelectorOnMainThread: deprecated and obscure
Hoping the response arrives on main: not guaranteed across iOS versions

index_layer.problem_summary: iOS deeplink/universal link parameters are URL-decoded twice, breaking encoded values like '+', '%26', or non-ASCII strings

resolution_layer.root_cause: System decodes the URL once before delivering it to the app; app then explicitly decodes again, corrupting characters that were intended literally
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Decode exactly once at the entry point; use URLComponents.queryItems which handles decoding internally; never call removingPercentEncoding on already-decoded strings
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Replace string?.removingPercentEncoding chain with URLComponents.queryItems[name]; add unit test for '+' and '%26' inputs
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Test deeplinks containing '+', '%26', '%20', and non-ASCII; assert raw value matches expected post-decode
abandoned_approaches: Disabling system decoding: not possible without proxy redirect
Catching the corrupted value and re-encoding: error-prone, doesn't generalize

index_layer.problem_summary: Android Handler holding outer Activity reference causes memory leak when Activity is destroyed before message delivery

resolution_layer.root_cause: Inner-class Handler implicitly references its enclosing Activity; pending messages keep the Handler alive, which keeps the Activity alive across rotation/destroy
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Use static inner class with WeakReference<Activity>; or remove all callbacks/messages in onDestroy; prefer Lifecycle-aware components (LifecycleObserver, Coroutine bound to lifecycleScope)
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Convert Handler to static + WeakReference; add removeCallbacksAndMessages(null) in onDestroy
resolution_layer.verification_plan: LeakCanary detection; rotate device repeatedly under instrumented test; profile with Memory Profiler
abandoned_approaches: Setting Activity reference to null: doesn't help, Handler still owns it
Catching destroyed-activity in callback: still leaks for the duration of the message queue

index_layer.problem_summary: RecyclerView ViewHolder reuse leaves stale state (selection, animation, async-loaded image) when scrolling

resolution_layer.root_cause: RecyclerView reuses ViewHolders across positions; if onBindViewHolder doesn't reset every visual property, the previous binding's state bleeds into the new row
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Reset every mutable property in onBindViewHolder (or onViewRecycled); cancel pending async work tied to the holder; use payloads to do partial diffs
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Add explicit reset in onBindViewHolder; cancel image-load request in onViewRecycled
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Scroll long list rapidly; observe selection / animation / image consistency; instrumented test with screenshot diff
abandoned_approaches: setIsRecyclable(false): defeats RecyclerView's purpose, hurts performance
Resetting only the visible delta: easy to forget a property, recurring bugs

index_layer.problem_summary: Async network callback fires after Activity/Fragment is destroyed, causing IllegalStateException or NPE

resolution_layer.root_cause: Network call started in onCreate doesn't observe lifecycle; when user navigates away, callback resumes on a dead Activity reference
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Bind async work to lifecycleScope / viewModelScope; cancel on destroy; use isAdded / isFinishing guards as last resort
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Replace raw callback with lifecycleScope.launch { ... }; or check fragment.isAdded before touching views
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Trigger network request, navigate away before response; verify no crash and correct memory profile
abandoned_approaches: try/catch around UI update: hides real lifecycle bug
setRetainInstance(true): legacy and deprecated for non-fragments

index_layer.problem_summary: React useEffect captures stale state via closure when dependency array is incomplete

resolution_layer.root_cause: useEffect closure binds to the props/state values at render time; omitting them from deps means the effect keeps running with old values
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Enable react-hooks/exhaustive-deps lint rule; include all referenced values in deps; use useRef + functional updater (setX(prev => ...)) when avoiding re-runs is required
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Add missing deps; or extract logic to useCallback / useReducer to stabilize references
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Manually trigger state change while effect-derived value is still in flight; add test asserting effect re-runs on dep change
abandoned_approaches: Using empty deps array deliberately: silently wrong in 90% of cases
Ignoring lint warning: tech debt that compounds

index_layer.problem_summary: Form onSubmit handler missing preventDefault causes full page reload, losing client-side state

resolution_layer.root_cause: Native form submission triggers a GET/POST and full page navigation; SPA assumes the handler intercepts but forgot to stop the default action
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Always call event.preventDefault() at the top of form submit handlers; consider extracting a useFormSubmit hook that does this implicitly
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Add e.preventDefault() as first line of submit handler; or replace <form onSubmit> with <button onClick> if no real form needed
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Test that submitting via Enter key in any input does not reload page; observe Network tab
abandoned_approaches: return false: works in jQuery, no-op in modern React
Disabling submit button after click: doesn't prevent Enter-key submission

index_layer.problem_summary: CORS or CSP misconfiguration blocks legitimate cross-origin or inline-asset requests in production but not in dev

resolution_layer.root_cause: Dev server is permissive (* origin); prod backend / CDN sets stricter Access-Control-Allow-Origin or Content-Security-Policy that excludes the actual origin
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Define exact allowed origins per environment; never use '*' with credentials; use CSP report-uri before enforcing; document the policy in runbook
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Update Access-Control-Allow-Origin to include prod domain; tighten CSP after report-only run
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Test from prod-like origin in staging; check browser console for blocked requests; review CSP report endpoint
abandoned_approaches: Disabling CORS in browser via flag: not a fix, masks the real issue
Using JSONP: deprecated, security-risky

index_layer.problem_summary: List endpoint executes one extra query per row (N+1) instead of joining or batching

resolution_layer.root_cause: ORM lazy-loads related entities one-at-a-time when iterating a result set; the join was assumed but not configured
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Use eager loading (selectinload/joinedload, JPA fetch join, Eloquent with()); add SQL log assertion in test; alarm on high query-per-request count in APM
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Add selectinload/JOIN FETCH for the related collection; verify SQL count drops from O(N) to O(1)
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Enable SQL logging in test; assert query count <= K for the endpoint; load-test with N=1000 to confirm linear -> constant
abandoned_approaches: Caching the list response: papers over the issue and creates staleness
Manually querying related table once: works but error-prone, doesn't compose

index_layer.problem_summary: Concurrent updates to the same resource race when no optimistic lock or row lock is used

resolution_layer.root_cause: Two requests read the same row, modify in memory, write back; the later write overwrites the earlier change without detection
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Add a version column with optimistic lock; or wrap in SELECT ... FOR UPDATE row-lock; for high-contention paths use atomic DB operations (UPDATE ... SET x = x + 1) instead of read-modify-write
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Add @Version column / WHERE version = ? AND row-id = ? clause; bump version on every update
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Concurrency test with N parallel writers asserting final value equals expected sum; chaos test with 50ms induced latency
abandoned_approaches: Application-level mutex: doesn't work across multiple instances
Adding sleep before write: hides but doesn't fix the race

index_layer.problem_summary: Hot cache key expires and many concurrent requests stampede the database (cache breakdown)

resolution_layer.root_cause: When a heavily-read key TTLs out, every concurrent request misses the cache and hits the underlying source simultaneously
resolution_layer.fix_strategy: Use single-flight / mutex on cache miss so only one request rebuilds; or use logical-expiry pattern (return stale + async refresh); or randomize TTL to avoid synchronized expiry
resolution_layer.patch_digest: Wrap cache.get with single-flight pattern; on miss only one goroutine/thread rebuilds, others wait
resolution_layer.verification_plan: Load test with key expiring during request burst; verify DB QPS does not spike beyond capacity; track cache_breakdown_count metric
abandoned_approaches: Long TTL only: data becomes stale and is hard to invalidate
Pre-warming cache on schedule: brittle, doesn't help unexpected hot keys
